ucs.title: [LLM] Volt Typhoon credential access — comsvcs.dll LSASS MiniDump + ntdsutil/wbadmin NTDS.dit extraction
ucs.description: Hunts Volt Typhoon's (Vanguard Panda / VOLTZITE / BRONZE SILHOUETTE) signature LOTL credential-access chain repeatedly observed by CISA/NSA/FBI in critical-infrastructure intrusions: dumping LSASS via `rundll32.exe comsvcs.dll, MiniDump` to harvest cleartext creds, then extracting the AD database from a domain controller using `ntdsutil 'ac i ntds' 'ifm' 'create full'` or `wbadmin start backup` of system state. This combination — exclusively native binaries, no malware drop — is uniquely emblematic of this actor.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_process_name values(Processes.process) as process from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=rundll32.exe Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" Processes.process="*MiniDump*") OR (Processes.process_name=ntdsutil.exe (Processes.process="*create full*" OR Processes.process="*ifm*" OR Processes.process="*ac i ntds*")) OR (Processes.process_name=wbadmin.exe Processes.process="*start*" Processes.process="*backup*" (Processes.process="*ntds*" OR Processes.process="*systemstate*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                                       // skip machine accounts
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
          and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
          and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump")                         // CISA AA24-038A LSASS dump
     or (FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe"
          and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("create full","ifm","ac i ntds")) // NTDS.dit IFM extraction
     or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe"
          and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("start","backup")
          and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ntds","systemstate"))         // wbadmin DC backup variant
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          GrandParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688                                                  // process create — needs cmdline GPO
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName endswith @"\rundll32.exe"
          and CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
          and CommandLine has "MiniDump")
     or (NewProcessName endswith @"\ntdsutil.exe"
          and CommandLine has_any ("create full","ifm","ac i ntds"))
     or (NewProcessName endswith @"\wbadmin.exe"
          and CommandLine has_all ("start","backup")
          and CommandLine has_any ("ntds","systemstate"))
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, LogonId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Volt Typhoon Credential Access via comsvcs.dll / ntdsutil / wbadmin
id: 9c1d2f3a-7e58-4b1a-9c8e-volttyphoon0001
status: experimental
description: Detects Volt Typhoon's signature LOTL credential-access chain — LSASS dump via rundll32+comsvcs.dll MiniDump and NTDS.dit extraction via ntdsutil IFM or wbadmin system-state backup
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-038a
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1017/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.t1003.003
  - attack.g1017
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  comsvcs_lsass:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'comsvcs.dll'
      - 'MiniDump'
  ntdsutil_ifm:
    Image|endswith: '\ntdsutil.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'create full'
      - 'ifm'
      - 'ac i ntds'
  wbadmin_ntds:
    Image|endswith: '\wbadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'start'
      - 'backup'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'ntds'
      - 'systemstate'
  condition: comsvcs_lsass or ntdsutil_ifm or wbadmin_ntds
falsepositives:
  - Sanctioned AD disaster-recovery operations (validate against change ticket)
  - Legitimate IFM media creation for new RODC promotion
level: high
ucs.rationale: CISA advisory AA24-038A (Feb 2024) and Microsoft's May-2023 Volt Typhoon report explicitly enumerate this exact triad of commands as Volt Typhoon's primary credential-access workflow — they avoid Mimikatz and other tooling that triggers AV, opting instead for native Microsoft binaries. The `rundll32.exe comsvcs.dll, MiniDump <pid> <out> full` invocation is functionally diagnostic of this actor; nt
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Volt Typhoon edge-router pivoting — netsh interface portproxy v4tov4 forwarding rule
ucs.description: Hunts the netsh `interface portproxy add v4tov4` command Volt Typhoon (VOLTZITE / Vanguard Panda) uses to convert compromised internal Windows hosts into relays that funnel C2 traffic out through their pre-positioned SOHO router botnet (KV-botnet — Cisco RV320s, NETGEAR ProSafe, Axis cams). This is one of the few non-LOLBin-discovery commands consistently attributed to this actor and creates a persistent IPv4-to-IPv4 forwarder that stages east-west pivoting and proxied egress.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent_process_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=netsh.exe Processes.process="*interface*" Processes.process="*portproxy*" (Processes.process="*add*" OR Processes.process="*v4tov4*" OR Processes.process="*v4tov6*" OR Processes.process="*v6tov4*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=process "listenport=(?<listenport>\d+).*?connectport=(?<connectport>\d+).*?connectaddress=(?<connectaddress>[\w\.\:]+)" | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName =~ "netsh.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_all ("interface","portproxy")             // only the forwarding subcommand
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("add","v4tov4","v4tov6","v6tov4")    // ignore 'show'/'reset'/'delete'
| extend ListenPort  = extract(@"(?i)listenport=(\d+)",  1, ProcessCommandLine),
         ConnectPort = extract(@"(?i)connectport=(\d+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine),
         ConnectAddr = extract(@"(?i)connectaddress=([\w\.:]+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine,
          ListenPort, ConnectPort, ConnectAddr,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentAccount = InitiatingProcessAccountName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith @"\netsh.exe"
| where CommandLine has_all ("interface","portproxy")
| where CommandLine has_any ("add","v4tov4","v4tov6","v6tov4")
| extend ListenPort  = extract(@"(?i)listenport=(\d+)",  1, CommandLine),
         ConnectPort = extract(@"(?i)connectport=(\d+)", 1, CommandLine),
         ConnectAddr = extract(@"(?i)connectaddress=([\w\.:]+)", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, ListenPort, ConnectPort, ConnectAddr, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Volt Typhoon netsh portproxy Forwarding Rule Creation
id: 7b8a9d4e-2c3f-46a8-bd1f-volttyphoon0002
status: experimental
description: Detects netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4 used by Volt Typhoon to relay C2 traffic through internal Windows hosts to compromised SOHO/edge routers (KV-botnet)
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-038a
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/05/24/volt-typhoon-targets-us-critical-infrastructure-with-living-off-the-land-techniques/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/08
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1090.001
  - attack.t1090.002
  - attack.g1017
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith: '\netsh.exe'
  selection_cmd_subsys:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'interface'
      - 'portproxy'
  selection_cmd_action:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'add'
      - 'v4tov4'
      - 'v4tov6'
      - 'v6tov4'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Developers tunnelling local services (rare on servers / DCs / production endpoints)
  - Legitimate dual-NIC bridging configured via documented runbook
level: high
ucs.rationale: Volt Typhoon's defining operational tradecraft is staging C2 through compromised SOHO routers (the KV-botnet that the FBI court-authorized takedown targeted in Jan 2024); the connector between the victim Windows host and that botnet is `netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4 listenport=<x> listenaddress=0.0.0.0 connectport=<y> connectaddress=<edge>`. CISA AA24-038A reproduces the exact command. Othe
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
